skill.name: laravel-expert
skill.description: Senior Laravel Engineer role for production-grade, maintainable, and idiomatic Laravel solutions. Focuses on clean architecture, security, performance, and modern standards (Laravel 10/11+).
skill.summary: Senior Laravel Engineer AI assistant for production-grade Laravel development with clean architecture, security best practices, and modern standards.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 13 static security findings are false positives. The scanner pattern-matched keywords without semantic understanding. The skill is a legitimate Laravel development assistant providing coding best practices with no malicious functionality.

low_findings.title: False Positive: HTTP client library pattern
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'HTTP client library' at line 81. Actual content: 'Never use request()->all() blindly' - This is Laravel's request() helper, not an HTTP client. No network risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 81 is about Laravel input validation, not HTTP client usage. The scanner misidentified the request() helper.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm patterns
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3, 41, 97, 163. These are normal Laravel best practices text, not cryptographic content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines contain generic terms like 'API', 'status codes', 'designing' - not cryptographic algorithms. Scanner false positive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System reconnaissance patterns
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'system reconnaissance' at lines 33, 69, 88, 90, 123, 128, 182. These contain Laravel best practices about patterns, static abuse, N+1 queries, raw queries, business logic, anti-patterns, and microservices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All lines contain standard Laravel development guidance. Scanner incorrectly matched keywords without understanding context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network reconnaissance pattern
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'network reconnaissance' at line 30. Actual content: 'Performance awareness' - not network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 30 is about performance optimization in Laravel, not network reconnaissance. Scanner false positive.

content.value_statement: Get expert Laravel development assistance for clean architecture, secure coding patterns, and modern Laravel 10/11+ best practices.
actual_capabilities: Generate production-ready Laravel code with clean architecture patterns
Design RESTful APIs following Laravel conventions and standards
Implement authentication and authorization using Laravel Sanctum and Policies
Optimize database queries and avoid N+1 problems with Eloquent
Refactor legacy Laravel code into maintainable service structures
Create proper validation logic using FormRequest classes
limitations: Cannot access external APIs or databases on behalf of the user
Cannot execute code or run Laravel commands directly
Does not provide hosting, deployment, or infrastructure setup
Limited to code generation and guidance, not actual project management

use_cases.title: New Laravel Feature Development
use_cases.description: Generate complete, production-ready Laravel features with proper architecture including controllers, services, migrations, and tests.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building new Laravel applications

use_cases.title: Code Review and Refactoring
use_cases.description: Get detailed code reviews with specific refactoring suggestions to improve Laravel code quality and follow framework conventions.

use_cases.title: API Design and Implementation
use_cases.description: Design and implement RESTful APIs using Laravel resources, proper status codes, pagination, and rate limiting.

prompt_templates.title: Create New Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete Laravel feature for [describe feature] including controller, service class, migration, and FormRequest validation. Follow clean architecture patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to build a new feature in Laravel

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Code
prompt_templates.prompt:
Review this Laravel code and identify issues. Provide a refactored version with explanation of improvements:

[paste code here]
prompt_templates.scenario: When improving legacy or poorly written Laravel code

prompt_templates.title: Design API Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a RESTful API endpoint for [describe resource] in Laravel. Include route definition, controller method, resource transformer, and validation.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement authentication for [describe use case] using Laravel Sanctum. Include middleware setup, policy registration, and controller logic.
prompt_templates.scenario: When adding authentication to Laravel applications

output_examples.input: Create a user registration API endpoint with email verification
output_examples.output:
I will design a complete registration API with controller, service, FormRequest, and API Resource. The implementation will include:

1. **Route Definition** - POST /api/register
2. **FormRequest** - RegistrationRequest with validation rules
3. **Controller** - RegistrationController with store method
4. **Service** - UserService for business logic
5. **API Resource** - UserResource for response formatting

Would you like me to generate the full code for any of these components?

output_examples.input: How do I optimize this Eloquent query to avoid N+1 problems?
output_examples.output:
To avoid N+1 queries, you should use eager loading. Here's how:

**Problem:** Loading posts with their authors in a loop causes N+1 queries.

**Solution:** Use with() for eager loading:

$posts = Post::with(['author', 'comments'])->get();

This loads all posts and their related authors/comments in just 2-3 queries instead of N+1. Use this pattern whenever you access relationships in loops.

best_practices: Keep controllers thin by moving business logic to service classes
Always use FormRequest classes for input validation instead of controller validation
Use route model binding for automatic model resolution and implicit route validation
anti_patterns: Avoid putting business logic directly in controllers - use service classes instead
Do not use request()->all() without explicit validation - always validate input
Avoid raw SQL queries unless absolutely necessary - use Eloquent query builder

faq.question: What Laravel versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports Laravel 10 and Laravel 11, focusing on modern standards and best practices for these versions.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Laravel Livewire?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on backend Laravel development. For Livewire-specific questions, you may need a specialized skill.

faq.question: Does this skill generate tests?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can generate unit tests and feature tests using Laravel's testing facilities when requested.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Laravel packages development?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can provide guidance on package development, service provider configuration, and package structure.

faq.question: Does this skill support Laravel Octane?
faq.answer: The skill can provide guidance on Octane-compatible code patterns, though the primary focus is on standard Laravel development.

faq.question: Can this skill help with database migrations?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill generates proper migrations with indexes, foreign keys, and rollback capabilities following Laravel conventions.
